hypothesis: A generative model trained to jointly predict both the crystal structure and its associated space group probability distribution directly from XRD patterns will exhibit systematically higher structure recovery rates than models conditioned on XRD alone, because embedding space group classification as an auxiliary prediction head during training forces the latent representation to encode symmetry-relevant features that reduce the effective dimensionality of the structure solution space, thereby mitigating the non-convex landscape problem identified in gradient-descent-based refinement.
